hero.title: Radiant Waves Media Morning Global News
hero.description: Sunday, June 28, 2026. Good morning. Bringing you today's top global headlines, FIFA World Cup knockout updates, politics, business, security and breaking news from around the world.

breaking: 🔴 FIFA World Cup Round of 32 fixtures officially confirmed after dramatic end to group stage
🔴 England defeat Panama 2-0 to finish top of Group L
🔴 DR Congo make history by reaching the World Cup knockout stage for the first time
🔴 Austria and Algeria both qualify after dramatic 3-3 draw, Iran eliminated
🔴 France prepare to face Sweden as Ousmane Dembélé leads Golden Boot race
🔴 Europe remains under extreme heatwave alerts
🔴 Nigeria sustains nationwide anti-terror operations against bandits and insurgents
🔴 Global markets prepare for a new trading week amid geopolitical uncertainty

featured.title: World Cup Knockout Stage Officially Begins
featured.content: The FIFA World Cup group stage has concluded, confirming the 32 teams that will compete for football's biggest prize. France, Brazil, Argentina, England, Spain, Belgium and Portugal remain among the favourites as the knockout phase begins across the United States, Canada and Mexico.

articles.title: France Face Sweden In Round Of 32
articles.description: France finished top of Group I after their outstanding 4-1 victory over Norway. Didier Deschamps' side now face Sweden in the knockout stage with Ousmane Dembélé arriving in sensational form after his historic hat-trick. 0

articles.title: Dembélé Among Golden Boot Leaders
articles.description: Ousmane Dembélé has emerged as one of the stars of the tournament with four goals in three matches. His hat-trick against Norway made him only the third French player to score a World Cup hat-trick. 1

articles.title: England Beat Panama 2-0
articles.description: England topped Group L after Jude Bellingham scored and assisted Harry Kane, whose goal made him England's all-time leading World Cup scorer with 11 goals. 2

articles.title: DR Congo Reach Knockout Stage For First Time
articles.description: DR Congo came from behind to defeat Uzbekistan 3-1, recording their first World Cup victory and booking a historic Round of 32 clash against England. 3

articles.title: Austria And Algeria Progress After Six-Goal Thriller
articles.description: Austria and Algeria played out an extraordinary 3-3 draw, with both nations qualifying while Iran were heartbreakingly eliminated despite drawing Egypt 1-1. 4

articles.title: Confirmed Round Of 32 Fixtures
articles.description: Major knockout ties include France vs Sweden, England vs DR Congo, Brazil vs Japan, Belgium vs Senegal, Portugal vs Croatia, Spain vs Austria, Argentina vs Cape Verde and Germany vs Paraguay. 5

articles.title: Middle East Diplomacy Continues
articles.description: Diplomatic negotiations and regional security remain major global priorities as governments monitor maritime security and humanitarian developments.

articles.title: Europe Faces Prolonged Heatwave
articles.description: France, Spain, Italy, Germany, Belgium and the United Kingdom continue experiencing exceptionally high temperatures, prompting health alerts and emergency measures.

articles.title: United States Hosts Knockout Phase
articles.description: Attention shifts to the Round of 32 as host cities across the United States prepare for the first knockout matches of the expanded FIFA World Cup.

articles.title: Canada Ready For Historic Knockout Match
articles.description: Co-host Canada prepares to face South Africa after progressing beyond the group stage for the first time on home soil. 6

articles.title: Nigeria Continues Nationwide Security Operations
articles.description: Military authorities sustained operations against Boko Haram, ISWAP, bandits and kidnapping gangs as insecurity remains one of the country's top priorities.

articles.title: Economic Reforms Remain National Focus
articles.description: Inflation, food security, tax reforms, infrastructure projects and electricity supply continue dominating discussions across Nigeria.

articles.title: African Nations Expand Trade Cooperation
articles.description: African governments continue strengthening regional trade, transport infrastructure and investment partnerships under the AfCFTA framework.

articles.title: Asia Continues Technology Growth
articles.description: China, Japan, South Korea and India continue investing in artificial intelligence, semiconductor manufacturing and advanced technology.

articles.title: Artificial Intelligence Investment Accelerates
articles.description: Governments and technology firms continue expanding investment in AI, cybersecurity, robotics and next-generation digital infrastructure.

articles.title: Markets Prepare For New Trading Week
articles.description: Global investors remain focused on inflation, central bank policies, oil prices and geopolitical risks heading into the new week.

breakingHistory: England defeated Panama 2-0 to finish top of Group L.
France prepare to face Sweden after topping Group I.
Ousmane Dembélé remains among the tournament's top scorers.
DR Congo reached the World Cup knockout stage for the first time.
Austria and Algeria qualified after an exciting 3-3 draw.
Iran were eliminated despite remaining unbeaten.
Europe continues battling an intense heatwave.
Global markets remain focused on inflation and oil prices.
The FIFA World Cup Round of 32 officially begins today.
